🇯🇵 🏦 Resona Bank Account Access Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is auctioning what i

sample_tweets.text: 🌐 Cisco Unified Communications Alleged CVE-2026-20045 RCE Exploit Source Code Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim to be source code and exploit material for CVE-2026-20045, a high-severity vulnerability affecting multiple Cisco Unified Communications products.  According to the forum post, the actor claims:  * Exploit targets CVE-2026-20045 * CVSS Score: 8.2 (High) * Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE) * Source code included * Exploit download available to forum members * Targets multiple Cisco Unified Communications platforms  Allegedly Affected Products:  * Cisco Unified Communications Manager (CUCM) * Cisco Unified CM Session Management Edition (SME) * Cisco Unified CM IM & Presence Service * Cisco Unity Connection * Cisco Webex Calling Dedicated Instance  Potential Risks:  * Remote code execution without authentication * Full system compromise of exposed communications infrastructure * Lateral movement into enterprise environments * Voice infrastructure disruption * Interception of communications and call management services * Persistence and privilege escalation opportunities following exploitation  Analyst Note: At this stage, the forum post only claims to provide exploit source code and no technical validation has been observed. However, Cisco Unified Communications platforms are widely deployed across government, healthcare, finance, and enterprise environments. Any publicly available or privately traded RCE exploit targeting these systems would significantly increase the likelihood of opportunistic exploitation and mass scanning activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Cisco

🇯🇵 🏦 Resona Bank Account Access Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is auctioning what is claimed to be access to a Japanese Resona Bank customer account containing approximately ¥6.9 million (~$45,000 USD).  According to the listing, the seller is offering a remote browser session with stored credentials, cookies, and autofill data rather than direct banking credentials.  Key Claims:  * Resona Bank customer account * Claimed balance: ¥6,900,000 (~$45,000 USD) * Access via remote browser session * Stored cookies and autofill data included * 12-hour underground auction * Transactions handled through forum escrow services  Notable Details:  * Seller claims full browser access to the victim environment * Physical banking card authentication reportedly required for transfers * Advertisement explicitly mentions social engineering as a potential method to bypass transfer restrictions * Windows Security listed as the only active endpoint protection  Threat Assessment:  * This appears to be an Initial Access/Banking Access sale rather than a traditional data leak. * The offering highlights how infostealer malware infections can provide criminals with authenticated browser sessions, cookies, saved credentials, and autofill information. * Even where financial institutions enforce additional authentication controls, threat actors frequently attempt account takeover through session hijacking, device trust abuse, or social engineering.  Potential Risks:  * Financial fraud * Account takeover attempts * Identity theft * Abuse of trusted browser sessions * Credential reuse against other services * Social engineering targeting the victim  Analyst Note: The growing underground market for browser-session access demonstrates that attackers increasingly value authenticated access more than passwords alone. Session cookies, saved credentials, and browser fingerprints can often provide immediate access to sensitive services even when multi-factor authentication is enabled.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Japan

🎮 GamasDeveloper Data Leak Claimed Following Alleged IDOR Vulnerability  A threat actor has published what they claim is data obtained from gamasdeveloper.com after exploiting an alleged insecure direct object reference (IDOR) vulnerability.  According to the post, the attackers state they were able to access files using document-specific identifiers that were not properly protected by authorization controls.  Threat Actor Claims:  * Access obtained through an alleged IDOR vulnerability * Sensitive document identifiers exposed through PDFs and images * Unauthorized access to internal files and records * Downloadable archive published publicly * Multiple contributors involved in the disclosure  Allegedly Exposed Content:  * Documentation * Invoices * Order records * Internal data files * Miscellaneous business records  Potential Risks:  * Exposure of customer and business information * Disclosure of financial records and invoices * Business intelligence leakage * Supply-chain and partner information exposure * Targeted phishing and social engineering attacks * Further exploitation if the underlying vulnerability remains unresolved  Assessment:  * The threat actor provides a technical explanation consistent with an IDOR-style access control weakness. * The claimed exposed data volume appears relatively small (approximately 882 KB), suggesting a limited disclosure rather than a large-scale database breach. * Independent verification is required to validate both the vulnerability and the authenticity of the leaked files.  Analyst Note: IDOR vulnerabilities remain one of the most common web application security issues. Even when authentication exists, predictable or exposed object identifiers can allow attackers to access documents and records belonging to other users if proper authorization checks are not enforced.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #DataLeak

🇦🇺 TEG User Dataset with 26M+ Profiles Allegedly Offered on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim to be a TEG (Ticketek Entertainment Group) user dataset containing more than 26 million records associated with an Australian entertainment and ticketing platform.  According to the listing, the dataset is being marketed for identity profiling, analytics, and user research purposes.  Allegedly Exposed Data:  * Usernames / nicknames * First names * Middle names (where available) * Last names * Gender * Dates of birth (where available) * Email addresses * User profile identifiers  Threat Actor Claims:  * More than 26 million records * Australia-based user population * Available in CSV, JSON, and Excel formats * Structured identity and profile dataset * Sample records provided  Potential Risks:  * Credential stuffing attacks * Account takeover attempts * Targeted phishing campaigns * Identity theft and impersonation * Marketing and profiling abuse * Cross-platform account correlation * Increased fraud against entertainment and ticketing customers  Assessment:  * The dataset appears to consist primarily of user profile information rather than financial records. * No evidence is provided linking the data directly to a recent compromise of TEG systems. * The structure resembles a large consumer identity dataset and requires independent validation. * The claimed scale of 26M+ records would represent a significant exposure if authentic.  Analyst Note: Entertainment and ticketing platforms often contain long-term customer records accumulated across multiple events, venues, and services. Even without payment information, large profile datasets can be highly valuable for phishing campaigns, identity correlation, and account takeover operations, particularly when users reuse credentials across platforms.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Australia

🇫🇷 France - Asso.gov Dataset with Banking Information Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a dataset associated with asso.gov containing approximately 227,000 records.  The listing specifically highlights the presence of banking information, including IBAN details, significantly increasing the potential impact if the data is authentic.  Allegedly Exposed Data:  * First and last names * Honorifics (Mr./Mrs.) * Professional titles and functions * Telephone numbers * Email addresses * Bank names * Branch/domiciliation information * IBAN numbers * BIC/SWIFT codes  Threat Actor Claims:  * 227,000 records * French government-related source ("asso.gov") * Banking information included * Dataset offered for sale for $300 * Sample records provided  Potential Risks:  * Financial fraud attempts * Business Email Compromise (BEC) * Invoice and payment redirection scams * Identity theft * Targeted phishing campaigns * Social engineering against association leaders and administrators * Abuse of exposed banking details in fraud operations  Assessment:  * The sample displayed appears to contain structured personal and banking information. * The relatively low sale price may indicate either widespread availability, questionable quality, or an attempt to quickly monetize the dataset. * Independent validation is required to determine authenticity and source attribution.  Analyst Note: While IBAN and BIC information alone generally cannot be used to directly withdraw funds, their exposure can significantly enhance the effectiveness of financial fraud, invoice manipulation, and impersonation campaigns when combined with names, phone numbers, and email addresses.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇳🇱 Netherlands Consumer Dataset with 7M+ Profiles Allegedly Offered on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising a large-scale Netherlands consumer dataset containing more than 7 million records. The seller describes the data as suitable for demographic analysis, geographic segmentation, and identity-based research.  According to the listing, the dataset is available in CSV, JSON, and Excel formats.  Allegedly Exposed Data:  * First names * Initials * Surnames * Dutch name prefixes (tussenvoegsel) * Gender * Date of birth * Email addresses * Primary and secondary phone numbers * Street names * House numbers * Postal codes * Municipalities * Provinces * Residence locations  Threat Actor Claims:  * More than 7 million records * Netherlands-wide coverage * Structured consumer demographic dataset * Multiple export formats available * Freshly released dataset  Potential Risks:  * Large-scale phishing campaigns * Identity theft and impersonation * Telephone and SMS fraud * Targeted social engineering attacks * Consumer profiling and surveillance * Physical address exposure * Enhanced credential-stuffing campaigns when combined with other breaches  Assessment:  * The dataset appears to contain extensive personally identifiable information (PII). * No clear source or victim organization is identified in the listing. * The structure resembles a compiled consumer intelligence or marketing database rather than a direct breach of a single organization. * Independent validation is required to determine the origin and authenticity of the records.  Analyst Note: Datasets that combine names, dates of birth, phone numbers, email addresses, and physical addresses are particularly valuable to cybercriminals because they enable highly targeted fraud campaigns. Even without financial data, such records can significantly improve the success rate of phishing, identity theft, and account recovery attacks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Netherlands

💳 Fast Payment System 2026 Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising a dataset described only as a "Fast Payment System 2026 base" on an underground forum.  The listing provides very limited information, making independent verification difficult. The actor claims the dataset contains approximately 10,000 unique records and is offering it for sale through escrow.  Observed Details:  * Claimed dataset: "Fast Payment System 2026" * Claimed volume: 10,000 unique records * Asking price: 600 (currency unspecified) * Escrow accepted * Sample provided but heavily obscured * Contact via Telegram  Assessment:  * Low confidence due to lack of technical details * No victim organization identified * No schema, field list, or verifiable sample disclosed * Could represent banking, payment processing, fintech, or payment gateway data, but this cannot currently be confirmed  Potential Risks If Authentic:  * Exposure of financial transaction information * Fraud and account takeover attempts * Financial identity theft * Targeted phishing against customers * Business email compromise and payment diversion schemes  Threat Actor Claims:  * Dataset is current for 2026 * Contains 10,000 unique records * Available through escrow-mediated sale  Analyst Note: Financial-themed listings with minimal evidence are common on underground forums. Until additional samples, victim attribution, or technical validation emerge, this should be treated as an unverified sale claim rather than a confirmed breach.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Finance

🤖 MyLovelyAI User Database Reappears on Underground Forums. Thanks for the DDW reference though 😄  A threat actor has reposted a processed CSV dataset allegedly originating from the MyLovelyAI breach, an incident previously reported in 2026 involving the NSFW AI companion platform.  According to the listing, the dataset contains 780,207 records and is being redistributed on underground forums.  Allegedly Exposed Data:  * Email addresses * Social media profile references * User-created AI prompts * Links to generated AI images * Discord usernames * X (Twitter) usernames  Potential Risks:  * Exposure of sensitive user interests and private conversations * Reputation and privacy concerns for affected users * Targeted phishing and social engineering campaigns * Blackmail and extortion attempts * Account correlation across multiple online identities * Credential stuffing against reused email accounts  Threat Actor Claims:  * 780,207 records included * Processed CSV format * Based on the previously disclosed MyLovelyAI breach * Dataset available for download and redistribution  Assessment:  * This appears to be a repost rather than a newly claimed compromise. * The listing references publicly known reporting and breach-tracking sources. * Redistribution significantly increases exposure risk because previously limited data becomes accessible to a broader criminal audience.  Analyst Note: AI companion and AI-generated content platforms present unique privacy risks. Unlike traditional breaches, exposed prompts and generated content can reveal highly personal interests, conversations, preferences, and behavioral patterns that may be leveraged for targeted harassment, extortion, or identity correlation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #MyLovelyAI

🇸🇪 Sweden - Safello Cryptocurrency Brokerage Transaction Data Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising what they claim to be transaction records from Safello, a Sweden-based cryptocurrency brokerage platform.  According to the listing, the dataset allegedly contains 807,977 transaction records associated with Safello users and cryptocurrency trading activity.  Allegedly Exposed Fields:  * Order identifiers * Transaction timestamps * BankID authentication references * First and last names * Street addresses * Email addresses * Mobile phone numbers * Dates of birth * Order types * Asset classes * Fiat transaction amounts (SEK) * Platform fees * Swish payment references * Blockchain transaction IDs * KYC status * Compliance flags  Potential Risks:  * Exposure of cryptocurrency investors and traders * Financial profiling of individuals * Targeted phishing and crypto-themed scams * SIM-swapping attempts against high-value users * Identity theft using KYC-related information * Physical security risks for individuals with significant crypto holdings * Money laundering and transaction tracing concerns  Threat Actor Claims:  * 807,977 transaction records * Cryptocurrency brokerage customer activity * KYC and compliance-related metadata included * Sample records allegedly provided as proof  Assessment:  * The dataset appears to contain structured transaction data rather than simple customer contact information. * Presence of BankID references, transaction identifiers, and blockchain transaction fields would significantly increase confidence if independently validated. * At this stage, the claims remain unverified.  Analyst Note: Cryptocurrency platforms represent high-value targets because breached transaction records can reveal both personal identities and financial behavior. If authentic, the combination of KYC information, transaction history, and blockchain references could enable highly targeted fraud, extortion, and cryptocurrency theft campaigns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Sweden

🇫🇷 French Police CHEOPS Search Access Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor is advertising access to searches within CHEOPS, a law enforcement information system reportedly used by French police and investigative units.  According to the listing, the actor claims to provide lookup services across multiple police and judicial databases rather than offering a database dump itself.  Alleged Available Searches:  * TAJ (Traitement des Antécédents Judiciaires) * FPR (Wanted/Persons of Interest Registry) * CR * ENQUETE (Investigations) * SIV (Vehicle Registration System) * Additional law enforcement datasets  Pricing Claimed:  * TAJ / FPR / CR / ENQUETE searches: $300 * SIV searches: $75  Potential Risks:  * Unauthorized access to police investigations * Exposure of sensitive law enforcement records * Intelligence gathering on individuals under investigation * Privacy violations involving French citizens * Support for criminal targeting, stalking, or fraud activities * Operational security risks for law enforcement agencies  Assessment:  * The post appears to advertise access-as-a-service rather than leaked data. * Screenshots are presented as proof, but independent verification is not currently available. * Such claims require careful validation, as underground forums frequently contain exaggerated or fraudulent offerings.  Analyst Note: Access to active police and investigative systems is generally far more valuable to threat actors than static leaked datasets because records can be queried in real time. If the claimed access is genuine, the risk would extend beyond privacy concerns to ongoing law enforcement operations and investigations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-21 08:08 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 위협은 AI 모델의 취약점을 직접 타겟팅하거나, AI 에이전트 및 공급망을 악용하여 인증 토큰을 탈취하는 정교한 형태로 진화하고 있습니다. 특히 `Gravity SMTP`와 같은 대중적인 플러그인 취약점 악용과 더불어, `EDR`을 무력화하는 전용 프레임워크를 사용하는 `RaaS` 그룹의 활동이 두드러집니다. 또한, `MITRE ATT&CK` 프레임워크를 기반으로 한 방어 체계 고도화와 함께, AI 모델의 보안성을 확보하기 위한 '주권형 AI 보안(Sovereign AI Security)' 전략이 기업의 핵심 과제로 부상하고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **The Gentlemen (Krebs on Security, WeLiveSecurity):** `GentleKiller` EDR 프레임워크를 사용하여 400개 이상의 보안 프로세스를 무력화하는 공격 수행.
*   **Sapphire Sleet (BlueNoroff) (BleepingComputer):** `Mastra AI` 공급망 공격을 통해 140개 이상의 `npm` 패키지를 감염시킨 북한 연계 해킹 그룹.
*   **Icarus (BleepingComputer):** `Klue`의 `OAuth` 토큰을 탈취하여 고객사의 `Salesforce` 환경에 무단 접근.
*   **TeamPCP (CyberScoop):** 오픈소스 소프트웨어 생태계를 타겟으로 하여 보안보다 속도를 우선시하는 개발 환경의 허점을 악용.
*   **Kimsuky (이스트시큐리티):** 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **북한발 공급망 공격:** `chai-as-init` 악성 `npm` 패키지가 `Chai.js` 플러그인으로 위장하여 배포됨 (이스트시큐리티).
*   **금융권 위협:** 2026년 5월 기준, 국내 금융권 대상 피싱 공격이 2.3으로 급증하며 초기 침투의 주요 경로로 활용됨 (AhnLab ASEC).
*   **스피어 피싱:** 보안 담당자를 대상으로 한 김수키(Kimsuky)의 정교한 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 (이스트시큐리티).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-4020:** Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin, API Key 노출, 악용 중 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/hackers-exploit-gravity-smtp-wordpress.html))
*   **CVE-2026-20230:** Cisco Unified CM, SSRF 및 파일 쓰기 취약점, RCE 체인 가능 ([YogSoth0/Twitter](https://twitter.com/YogSoth0/status/2068438897688707306))
*   **CVE-2026-56345:** AVideo Meet Plugin, 인증 우회 및 RCE 가능 ([infoflowcloud/Twitter](https://twitter.com/infoflowcloud/status/2068467917251878975))
*   **CVE-2025-71331:** Flowise, XSS 취약점, 입력 필터링 미흡 ([infoflowcloud/Twitter](https://twitter.com/infoflowcloud/status/2068467989909799171))
*   **CVE-2026-56227:** Capgo, SSRF 취약점, 내부 주소 접근 가능 ([infoflowcloud/Twitter](https://twitter.com/infoflowcloud/status/2068467997971288378))

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE:**
    *   CVE-2026-4020 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/hackers-exploit-gravity-smtp-wordpress.html))
    *   CVE-2026-20230 ([YogSoth0](https://twitter.com/YogSoth0/status/2068438897688707306))
    *   CVE-2026-56345 ([infoflowcloud](https://twitter.com/infoflowcloud/status/2068467917251878975))
    *   CVE-2025-71331 ([infoflowcloud](https://twitter.com/infoflowcloud/status/2068467989909799171))
    *   CVE-2026-56227 ([infoflowcloud](https://twitter.com/infoflowcloud/status/2068467997971288378))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Gravity SMTP 패치:** 사용 중인 WordPress 환경에서 `Gravity SMTP` 플러그인을 최신 버전으로 즉시 업데이트하고 API Key 유출 여부를 점검하십시오.
*   **OAuth 및 서비스 계정 감사:** `Klue` 사례와 같이 `OAuth` 토큰을 사용하는 연동 인프라의 권한을 재검토하고, 불필요한 레거시 자격 증명을 폐기하십시오.
*   **공급망 보안 강화:** `npm` 등 오픈소스 패키지 사용 시 의존성 패키지의 무결성을 검증하고, `chai-as-init`과 같은 악성 패키지 포함 여부를 스캔하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수:** 8/10
*   **근거:** RaaS 그룹의 EDR 무력화 기술 고도화와 AI 공급망 공격이 결합되어 기업의 보안 운영 속도를 상회하는 위협이 지속되고 있음.

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 14,814개 (입력 13,374 / 출력 1,440)
📈 세션 누적: 14,814 / 1,000,000 (1.5%) | 세션 잔여 추정 985,186개
